title: 10 best herbal teas for oily skin

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, promoting a clearer complexion.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in aloe vera tea help combat acne-causing bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily skin, reducing the risk of breakouts.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to premature aging and oil production, while also soothing and calming the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to regulate and balance the body's oil production. The tea is rich in aloe vera gel, which contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and unclog pores, thereby minimizing the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, aloe vera teas have anti-septic and anti-bacterial properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloein helps by acting as an astringent, reducing sebum production and thereby controlling oily skin.
Polysaccharides (such as glucomannan and acemannan): These components help by forming a protective barrier on the skin, reducing oil absorption and regulating sebum production.

Phenolic compounds (such as aloesin): Aloesin helps by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a key contributor to oily skin and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, which helps to balance and regulate oil production in the skin.
Stems: Aloe vera stems are utilized for their gel-like substance, which can help to reduce inflammation and control sebum production, benefiting oily skin.
Leaves: (Again) Aloe vera leaves are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing redness and soothing irritated skin associated with oily skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh aloe vera leaves of medium size from the garden or store-bought aloe vera gel.
Wash the aloe vera leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or residue.
Peel the outer skin of the aloe vera leaves and chop the inner gel into small pieces weighing about 2 ounces.
Steep the aloe vera gel in a large pot of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active ingredients.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve warm.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass tea contains compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can reduce inflammation and prevent acne-causing bacteria from multiplying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants in lemongrass tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to calm redness and swelling associated with oily skin, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Astringent properties: Lemongrass tea has astringent compounds that can help to constrict pores and reduce sebum production, leaving the skin looking clearer and less shiny.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, lemongrass tea's antiseptic properties help to eliminate bacteria that can clog pores and exacerbate oily skin, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion. Regular consumption of lemongrass tea may also help to balance the skin's natural pH.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A terpene compound found in lemongrass, citral has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oil production and prevent clogged pores, thereby helping to control oily skin.

Limonene: A terpene compound in lemongrass, limonene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce sebum production and prevent acne, ultimately contributing to a reduction in oily skin.

Geraniol: A terpene compound with astringent and antiseptic properties, geraniol can help to reduce oil production, prevent bacterial growth, and soothe inflammation, all of which can contribute to healthier and less oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for oily skin due to their high lemongrass oil content, which helps to reduce sebum production and balance skin pH.
Rhyzomes: Employed in teas to combat oily skin as they contain compounds that help to regulate oil glands and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Utilized in teas for their ability to help reduce oiliness and soothe the skin, thanks to the presence of lemongrass oil and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water for infusion.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5 minutes to release their herbal properties and flavors.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids from the tea.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to dissolve the sweetener in the tea.
Serve the tea hot or iced and consume immediately to enjoy the benefits of the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm tea has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea may help reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, as well as soothe any irritation or discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea is rich in antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals on the skin's surface, which can damage skin cells and contribute to oily skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and luteolin, help to balance the skin's pH and reduce sebum production. This results in a decrease in oiliness and a more even skin tone. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis tea's soothing and calming effects can also help to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with oily skin, promoting a healthier and more balanced complexion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has been shown to have a balancing effect on the skin's natural oil production, reducing sebum secretion and preventing clogged pores.
Linalool: A terpene that possesses anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to reduce redness and tighten the skin, which can be beneficial for controlling oily skin.
Limonene: A terpene that has been found to exhibit a drying effect on the skin, which can help to reduce the appearance of oily skin and prevent acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have a balancing effect on the skin, helping to reduce oil production and prevent acne.
Flowers: They have a soothing and calming effect, reducing inflammation and excess oil production, which can help to alleviate oily skin issues.
Stems: They contain astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, making them a popular choice for teas addressing oily skin concerns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from the garden or a trusted supplier 2-3 times a week during the summer months.
Dry the freshly harvested melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves per 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried melissa officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the herbal tea and enjoy warm or chilled as a calming and relaxing beverage.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to oily skin and acne.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in rosemary tea help to shrink the appearance of pores, reducing their size and visibility, which can make oily skin appear less greasy.
Antibacterial properties: Rosemary tea's antibacterial agents help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, including Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a common cause of acne and oily skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, helps to reduce sebum production and purify the skin. This leads to a decrease in oiliness and a more balanced skin pH, resulting in a smoother and clearer complexion. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: A diterpenoid compound that helps reduce oil production by inhibiting the activity of the sebaceous gland and thereby controlling sebum secretion.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic diterpene with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oil production and prevent clogged pores, leading to a balanced skin condition.

Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria that may contribute to oily skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are often used to make teas for oily skin due to their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance skin oil and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make teas for oily skin as they have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing oil production.
Buds: The buds are used to make teas for oily skin due to their astringent properties, which help to tighten pores and reduce oil production, promoting a balanced skin tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from mature branches in the morning.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 35c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Measure out 2g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves for each 250ml of water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their oils and flavor.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and oily skin.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to inflammation and oil production.
Astringent properties: Sage tea's astringent properties help to reduce the appearance of pores and tighten the skin, which can make it look less oily and more balanced.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, teas helps with oily skin because of its antiseptic and astringent properties. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores, thereby controlling oily skin.
Salvianolic acid B: A water-soluble phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties, which help to reduce sebum production and prevent skin irritation associated with oily skin.

Ursolic acid: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce sebum production, prevent skin irritation, and promote a balanced skin pH, ultimately controlling oily skin.

Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain a compound called salvin, which has been shown to have astringent properties, helping to dry out the skin and reduce oiliness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Measure 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their flavor and oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves before serving.

Steep 1-2 teaspoons of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day.

Grind 2-5 grams of dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered plant material in a cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving and consuming the liquid immediately.

Steep 2-3 teaspoons of the harvested flowers in 1 quart of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container immediately after steeping.

Allow the tea to steep for eight to ten minutes before straining with a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink two cups of the cooled tea once daily for three weeks to alleviate oily skin.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to oily skin and acne.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in thyme tea help to constrict pores and dry up excess oil, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling sebum production.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to inflammation, which can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria, making them effective in tea form to soothe oily skin.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin, while also providing antioxidant benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or purchase dried thyme from a reputable supplier.
Measure one teaspoon of dried thyme or two teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its therapeutic properties and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before applying it to the skin as a toner or compress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as citronellal and geraniol, help to reduce sebum production and tighten pores, leaving the skin looking more balanced and refined. Additionally, coriander tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate acne and other oily skin issues, promoting a healthier and clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Coriander tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and clogged pores, reducing the appearance of acne and oiliness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in coriander tea may help reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, while also soothing irritation and calming the skin.

Astringent and antibacterial properties: Coriander tea's astringent and antibacterial compounds can help tighten pores, reducing oil production and preventing bacterial growth that can lead to acne and other skin issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, coriander roots can help reduce inflammation and calm acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Coriander seeds contain linoleic acid, which helps regulate sebum production and reduce oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriander leaves and wash them thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried coriander leaves for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle and add the measured coriander leaves allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining only the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it and drinking it chilled throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The menthol in peppermint helps to tighten pores, reducing their appearance and absorbing excess oil, while the antioxidants present in the herb help to combat inflammation and reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can help to calm and soothe irritated skin, leaving it feeling refreshed and balanced.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to oily skin and acne.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in peppermint tea, like menthol and menthone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that can clog pores and exacerbate oily skin.

Astringent and cooling properties: Peppermint tea's astringent and cooling effects help constrict pores, reduce sebum production, and soothe acne-prone skin, making it an effective remedy for oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used in tea blends to help balance oil production and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves have natural astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce sebum production, making them an effective ingredient for oily skin teas.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for five to seven minutes, releasing their oils and compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Consume two cups of the peppermint tea daily, ideally in the morning and evening to reduce oil production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with oily skin because its antibacterial properties help to reduce excess oil production, while its antifungal properties soothe and calm the skin. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English Lavender tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to oily skin and acne.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried laurus nobilis leaves and lavandula angustifolia flowers in equal proportions measuring one teaspoon each.
Combine the herbs with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot for an infusion time of five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup discarding any solids and herbs.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional two to three minutes before serving hot or chilled as desired.
Consume one to two cups of lavandula angustifolia tea per day for a minimum period of four weeks to notice improved oily skin conditions.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal teas are acne, rosacea, and eczema. Certain herbs in herbal teas, such as green tea, chamomile, and peppermint, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with these conditions. These teas can also help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce sebum production, making them a popular alternative to traditional skincare treatments.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily skin are infused drinks made from herbs that help regulate oil production and reduce skin inflammation. They work by balancing the skin's natural pH and soothing irritation, leading to a healthier and more balanced complexion. Some of the best herbal teas for oily skin include Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Salvia officinalis, Camellia sinensis, Calendula officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Silybum marianum, Melissa officinalis, and Cymbopogon citratus. If left untreated, oily skin can lead to acne, scarring, and low self-esteem, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with oily skin is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and green tea. Peppermint tea helps to reduce oil production and calm inflammation, while chamomile tea soothes and regulates the skin's natural balance. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, aids in purifying the skin and reducing pores. Drinking this tea blend regularly can help to control oily skin, reduce acne, and promote a healthy, balanced complexion. It's also a natural and gentle alternative to harsh skincare products.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent properties, which help to reduce sebum production and tighten pores. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds also work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin. Additionally, the tea's ability to regulate the body's hormonal balance can help to control oil production, leading to a more balanced and healthy complexion. This makes Rosmarinus officinalis tea a popular natural remedy for managing oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with oily skin by reducing inflammation and fighting off free radicals, which contribute to clogged pores and acne.
Carnosic acid: A terpene that helps to balance sebum production, carnosic acid reduces oiliness by regulating the body's natural oil production.
Carnosol: This diterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of oily skin and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in antioxidants and essential oils, which help to balance skin pH and reduce oil production.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, a natural astringent that helps to tighten pores and reduce excess oil.
Flowers: Rich in antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to control acne and prevent bacterial growth that can contribute to oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning when they are most fragrant and dry.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and steep them in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to balance the flavor and 1/4 teaspoon of lemon juice.
Serve the tea immediately and store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to regulate and balance the skin's oil production. The tea contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores. Additionally, aloe vera's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and inflammation, further contributing to a more balanced and healthy skin complexion. This results in a smoother, clearer, and more even-toned complexion.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: It helps with oily skin by acting as an astringent, reducing sebum production, and promoting skin cell turnover.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound helps by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, which can contribute to oily skin.
Chrysophanic acid: It helps by modulating the skin's natural pH levels, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antibacterial properties, all of which can help control oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis for teas as they contain high levels of aloe vera gel, which helps to balance the skin's natural oil production and reduce inflammation.

Roots: The roots of Aloe barbadensis contain saponins and other compounds that can help to regulate oil production and reduce sebum in the skin, making them a popular choice for teas aimed at oily skin.
(Optional, but commonly used) Stems: The stems of Aloe barbadensis contain similar compounds to the leaves and roots, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 leaves of aloe barbadensis in the morning when they are at their highest gel content.
Peel the leaves and cut them into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their gel.
Soak the aloe pieces in 2 cups of hot water at 95-100 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Refrigerate the tea in an airtight container for 2-3 hours before serving chilled.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with oily skin because it has a balancing effect on the skin's natural oil production. The tea's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce acne and soothe irritated skin. The flavonoids present in the tea also have a calming effect on the skin, reducing stress and inflammation that can contribute to oily skin. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to tone and tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an antibacterial agent, reducing the growth of bacteria that can contribute to oil production and clogged pores.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses antioxidant properties, helping to protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate oily skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for oily skin because they are known for their balancing properties and ability to regulate oil production.
Flowers: Used to make teas for oily skin because they have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent breakouts.
Stems: Used to make teas for oily skin because they contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of moisture.
Rinse the harvested flowers gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried or 2 teaspoons of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the flowers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas helps with oily skin because it contains salvinic acids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and prevent overproduction of sebum, leading to a decrease in oiliness. The tea's ability to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce oxidative stress also contributes to its skin-soothing effects. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used to calm and regulate the body's natural functions, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Helps to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in the skin, thereby controlling oily skin.
Thujone: Exerts an antiseborrheic effect, which can help to reduce the production of sebum and alleviate symptoms of oily skin.
Salvianolic acid: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, leading to a more balanced skin condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are the most commonly used part to make teas for oily skin because they contain antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis are sometimes used in tea blends for oily skin because they contain antioxidants that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation.
Buds: The buds of Salvia officinalis are used in some herbal teas for oily skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the appearance of pores and prevent breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Measure out 2 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves per 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5 to 10 minutes then strain the mixture using a cheesecloth.
Drink the tea immediately while it is still warm and fragrant.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas helps with oily skin because of its unique combination of antioxidants and polyphenols. These compounds help to regulate sebum production, reducing the amount of oil released onto the skin's surface. remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help reduce oily skin by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that contributes to the production of androgens, which can stimulate oil gland activity.
Quinic acid derivatives: Have astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, thereby minimizing the appearance of oily skin.

Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin, while also modulating the skin's natural barrier function to prevent excessive oil production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Camellia sinensis are often used to make green tea, which is known for its astringent properties that help to reduce oil and balance skin tone.
Buds: The young buds of Camellia sinensis are used to make white tea, which has natural antibacterial properties that help to control oil production and prevent acne.
Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis are also used to make oolong tea, which is partially fermented and contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and regulate sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of loose camellia sinensis leaves for every 8 oz of water used.
Heat 8 oz of fresh water to a temperature of 200-212°f in a teapot.
Steep the camellia sinensis leaves in the heated water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool to a temperature that is suitable for consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with oily skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and balance the skin's natural pH. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of fresh calendula officinalis flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers at any time of day.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours or air-dry them for 1-2 weeks.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the desired tea strength is reached and the liquid has cooled.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then drink the remaining liquid immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency for maximum flavor and aroma.
Gently rinse the thymus leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Steep one teaspoon of fresh thymus leaves in one cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the thymus infusion into a cup using a fine mesh sieve to remove the leaves.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried silybum marianum seeds in autumn when the plant is mature.
Rinse the seeds gently with filtered water to remove any impurities and debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of silybum marianum seeds in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning to preserve their fragrance and flavor.

Combine 1 tablespoon of fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to create tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems from a healthy plant at dawn to ensure optimal oil content.
Rinse the harvested leaves and stems under cool running water to remove dirt and impurities immediately.
Chop the leaves and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area for infusion.
Steep 5g of the chopped leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids to enjoy the tea.
